title: User-Centered Design Iterative Process
description: Algorithm showing the iterative UX design cycle
code:
PROCEDURE IterativeUXDesign
REPEAT
 // Phase 1: Research
 ConductUserResearch()
 AnalyzeUserData()
 
 // Phase 2: Define
 CreatePersonas()
 DefineUserJourneys()
 
 // Phase 3: Ideate
 BrainstormSolutions()
 CreateUserFlows()
 
 // Phase 4: Prototype
 DesignWireframes()
 BuildInteractivePrototypes()
 
 // Phase 5: Test
 ConductUsabilityTesting()
 GatherUserFeedback()
 
 // Iterate based on feedback
 IdentifyImprovements()
 UpdateDesigns()
 
UNTIL UserSatisfactionTargetMet OR MaxIterationsReached
END PROCEDURE
explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the iterative nature of UX design, showing how each phase feeds into the next with continuous user feedback integration.

complexity.time: O(n) iterations based on testing cycles

title: SE-UX Collaboration Workflow
description: Process showing how SE and UX teams collaborate
code:
PROCEDURE SE_UXCollaboration
// Initialize project with joint session
JointRequirementsSession()
CreateSharedProjectGoals()

// Parallel work with continuous sync
PARALLEL_EXECUTE
 // Engineering Tasks
 DefineTechnicalConstraints()
 PlanSystemArchitecture()
 IdentifyFeasibilityIssues()
 
 // UX Tasks
 ConductUserResearch()
 DesignUserInterface()
 CreatePrototypes()
END_PARALLEL

// Regular sync points
FOR each Sprint do
 JointDesignReview()
 ResolveTechnicalFeasibilityIssues()
 UpdateDesignBasedOnTechnicalInput()
 ConductUsabilityTesting()
 IntegrateFeedbackIntoDevelopment()
END_FOR

// Final validation
JointUserAcceptanceTesting()
FinalizeProductWithBothPerspectives()
END PROCEDURE
explanation: This algorithm demonstrates how Software Engineering and UX teams work in parallel while maintaining regular synchronization to ensure both technical and user requirements are met.

complexity.time: O(s) where s is number of sprints
complexity.space: O(t) where t is team size

title: Usability Testing Protocol
description: Procedure for conducting usability testing sessions
code:
PROCEDURE ConductUsabilityTesting
INPUT: TestTasks, SuccessCriteria

// Preparation Phase
SelectTestParticipants(TargetUserProfiles)
CreateTestScript()
SetupTestEnvironment()

// Testing Phase
FOR each Participant do
 WelcomeAndIntroduce()
 GatherDemographicInfo()
 
 FOR each Task in TestTasks do
 ExplainTaskToParticipant()
 StartTimer()
 ParticipantAttemptsTask()
 StopTimer()
 Record(TaskCompletionStatus, TimeTaken, Errors)
 
 IF Participant.RequestsHelp THEN
 RecordHelpCount()
 END_IF
 
 // Think-aloud protocol
 ParticipantVerbalizesThoughts()
 END_FOR
 
 GatherSubjectiveFeedback()
 ThankParticipant()
END_FOR

// Analysis Phase
CalculateTaskCompletionRate()
CalculateAverageTaskTime()
IdentifyCommonIssues()
GenerateUsabilityReport()

RETURN UsabilityReport
END PROCEDURE
explanation: This pseudocode outlines the systematic approach to usability testing, capturing both quantitative metrics and qualitative feedback.

title: Wireframe Layout Algorithm
description: Basic algorithm for creating wireframe structure
code:
PROCEDURE CreateWireframe
INPUT: ContentRequirements, UserFlows

// Define grid structure
GridSystem = InitializeGrid(Columns=12, Gutter=20px)

// Header section
Header = CreateSection(GridColumns=1to12)
AddElement(Header, Logo)
AddElement(Header, NavigationMenu)

// Main content area
MainContent = CreateSection(GridColumns=1to8)
FOR each ContentBlock in ContentRequirements do
 Element = CreatePlaceholder(ContentBlock.Type)
 Element.Dimensions = CalculateSize(ContentBlock.Priority)
 AddElement(MainContent, Element)
END_FOR

// Sidebar if needed
IF HasSecondaryContent THEN
 Sidebar = CreateSection(GridColumns=9to12)
 AddElements(Sidebar, SecondaryContent)
END_IF

// Footer section
Footer = CreateSection(GridColumns=1to12)
AddElement(Footer, Copyright)
AddElement(Footer, FooterLinks)

// Output wireframe structure
Wireframe = AssembleSections(Header, MainContent, Sidebar, Footer)
RETURN Wireframe
END PROCEDURE
explanation: This algorithm demonstrates the systematic approach to creating wireframes by organizing content within a grid structure, prioritizing elements based on content requirements.

complexity.time: O(c) where c is number of content blocks

title: A/B Testing Implementation
description: Algorithm for conducting A/B tests on design variations
code:
PROCEDURE ConductABTest
INPUT: VariantA, VariantB, TargetMetric, Duration

// Setup
TrafficSplit = 50 // 50% each variant
InitializeTracking()

// Distribution phase
FOR each UserVisit do
 RandomValue = GenerateRandom(0,100)
 IF RandomValue < TrafficSplit THEN
 ShowVariant(VariantA)
 TrackUser(VariantA)
 ELSE
 ShowVariant(VariantB)
 TrackUser(VariantB)
 END_IF
END_FOR

// Collect data during test duration
Wait(Duration)

// Analysis
DataA = RetrieveMetrics(VariantA, TargetMetric)
DataB = RetrieveMetrics(VariantB, TargetMetric)

// Statistical analysis
Significance = CalculateStatisticalSignificance(DataA, DataB)
ConversionDiff = CalculateDifference(DataA, DataB)

// Decision
IF Significance >= 0.95 THEN
 IF DataA.Performance > DataB.Performance THEN
 Winner = VariantA
 ELSE
 Winner = VariantB
 END_IF
 DeployWinner(Winner)
ELSE
 ContinueTesting() // Need more data
END_IF

RETURN Winner, StatisticalAnalysis
END PROCEDURE
explanation: This pseudocode shows how A/B testing works by randomly distributing users between variants and analyzing which performs better based on statistical significance.

complexity.time: O(n) where n is number of visitors
complexity.space: O(v) where v is data collected per visitor
